questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.comment: Paris has been the capital of France since 508 AD.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.comment: Mars looks red because of iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.question: What is the largest mammal in the world?
questions.comment: The blue whale can reach up to 30 meters long and 200 tonnes.

questions.question: Who wrote the play 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.comment: Shakespeare wrote it early in his career, around 1594-1596.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.comment: Au comes from the Latin word for gold, 'aurum'. Ag is silver.

questions.question: In which year did World War II end?
questions.comment: WWII ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany (May) and Japan (September).

questions.question: What is the smallest prime number?
questions.comment: 2 is the smallest and the only even prime number.

questions.question: Which language has the most native speakers worldwide?
questions.comment: Mandarin Chinese has roughly 920 million native speakers.
